zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth de Ros於大約1366年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，她的父親Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt31歲，母親Beatrice Stafford27歲。她於1387年與Thomas Clifford在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德布盧結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1424年3月26日享年59歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭維斯特莫藍德阿波萊比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth de Ros于大约1366年出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，她的父亲Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt31岁，母亲Beatrice Stafford27岁。她于1387年与Thomas Clifford在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德布盧结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1424年3月26日享年59岁，埋葬在英国英格兰維斯特莫藍德阿波萊比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth de Ros was born about 1366, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt, was 31 and her mother, Beatrice Stafford, was 27. She married Thomas Clifford in 1387, in Brough, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 26 March 1424, at the age of 59, and was buried in Appleby, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth de Ros est née environ 1366, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Beatrice Stafford, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Clifford en 1387, à Brough, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 26 mars 1424, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterrée à Appleby, Westmorland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1387 in Brough, Westmorland, England, United Kingdom Thomas Clifford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 26. ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Rosが約1366年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de Ros, Kntは31歳，母Beatrice Staffordは27歳であった。1387年にイギリスイギリスウエストモーランドブロウでThomas Cliffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1424年3月26日に59歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスウエストモーランドアップレビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth de Ros(이)가 대략 1366년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice Stafford(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1387년 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 브루그h에서 Thomas Clifford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1424년 3월 26일 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트모를랜드 애플레비에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Clifford em 1387, em Brough, Westmorland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth de Ros родилась около 1366 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas de Ros, Knt было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Beatrice Stafford, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Clifford в 1387 г. в местности броую, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 26 марта 1424 г. в возрасте 59 лет и была похоронена в местности эпплеби, вестморланд, Англия, Великобритания.
